c74object.name: multi_track
c74object.digest: Neural network audio stem prediction directly to/from buffer~
c74object.description:
Reads audio context directly from a multichannel buffer~, sends it to a Python neural network server over OSC/UDP for inference, and writes the predicted stems back into the same buffer.
Buffer coordinate system: T = context window size in samples (full model receptive field). p = percentage — prediction window as a fraction of T. w = pr_win_mul (-1, 0, 1) — which window slot to write into relative to cursor. Read context: [curr - p*T → curr] (p*T samples before the cursor). Write prediction: [curr + w*p*T → curr + (w+1)*p*T].

objarg.name: buffer-name
objarg.digest: Name of the multichannel buffer~ to read from and write to
objarg.description: Pass the buffer name as the first argument, followed by stem names: multi_track mybuffer bass drums guitar piano. Stem names must match the channel count of the buffer. Can also be set at runtime via the set_buffer message. Max attribute arguments (e.g. @verb 1) may follow the stem names.

objarg.name: stem-names
objarg.digest: Ordered stem names matching buffer channels (e.g. bass drums guitar piano)
objarg.description: One symbol per buffer channel. Used to label stems in the console, match incoming prediction OSC addresses, and name outgoing context planes in send_mode 1. Stop parsing at the first @ attribute argument. Example: multi_track mybuf bass drums guitar piano @verb 1

inlet.digest: Messages: predict, set_buffer, T, fade, send_mode, percentage, pr_win_mul, ...

outlet.digest: Bang — fires when the predicted stem has been written to the buffer

method.name: predict
method.digest: Trigger a prediction at the given cursor position (in samples)
method.description: Reads p*T samples of context from [curr - p*T → curr] in the buffer, sends them to the Python server, waits for the prediction, and writes the result back to [curr + w*p*T → curr + (w+1)*p*T]. A bang is output when the write is complete. Prediction is skipped if curr = 0 (no past context available).

method.name: set_buffer
method.digest: Bind to a multichannel buffer~ and optionally name the stems
method.description: Binds the object to the named buffer~. Optionally, pass one symbol per channel after the buffer name to label the stems: set_buffer mybuf bass drums guitar piano. Stem names must match the buffer channel count, otherwise a warning is posted. If no names are given, stems are referred to as ch0, ch1, etc.

method.name: T
method.digest: Set the model context window size in samples
method.description: T is the full receptive field of the neural network model (e.g. 264600 for 6 seconds at 44100 Hz). Must be set before the first predict call.

method.name: fade
method.digest: Set the crossfade overlap as a fraction of the sample rate (e.g. 0.02)
method.description: A linear crossfade of ratio * samplerate samples is applied at the start of the write region, blending the new prediction with the existing audio. Example: 0.02 at 44100 Hz gives ~882 samples of crossfade. Set to 0.0 for hard replacement. Synced to the server on load_model.

method.name: send_mode
method.digest: Set context send mode: 0 = sum to /context, 1 = separate stems by name
method.description: 0 (sum): All non-predicted channels are summed into one plane and sent as /context. Faster — sends 1/N the data. 1 (separate): Each non-predicted channel is sent as its own plane using the stem name as the OSC address (e.g. /bass, /drums). More flexible — allows models that take individual stems as input.

method.name: percentage
method.digest: Set the prediction window as a fraction of T (0.0 – 1.0)
method.description: p controls the size of data exchanged per step: p*T samples are both sent as context and received as prediction. Example: T=264600, p=0.25 gives a 66150-sample (1.5s) prediction window per step.

method.name: pr_win_mul
method.digest: Set the window slot: -1, 0, or 1 (write position relative to cursor)
method.description: w determines where the prediction is written relative to the cursor: -1 = one prediction window before cursor, 0 = at cursor, 1 = one prediction window after cursor. Write region = [curr + w*p*T → curr + (w+1)*p*T].

method.name: predict_instruments
method.digest: One-hot vector selecting which stems to predict (1 = predict, 0 = pass through)
method.description: Sends one integer flag per stem (in the order defined by set_buffer). The server generates only the stems with flag = 1; the others are passed through unchanged. Number of flags must match the stem count. Example with 4 stems: predict_instruments 1 0 0 0 predicts the first stem only.

method.name: server
method.digest: Start (1) or stop (0) the Python server
method.description: Launches the server using the command set via set_command. The model is loaded automatically once the server reports ready.

method.name: set_command
method.digest: Set the shell command used to launch the Python server
method.description: Supports local commands and SSH remote commands. The server IP is parsed automatically from --server_ip or ssh user@host. Example: set_command python server.py --server_ip 192.168.1.100

method.name: load_model
method.digest: Push all parameters to the server and trigger model load
method.description: Sends packet_size, percentage, pr_win_mul, and predict_instruments to the server, then sends the load_model command. Called automatically after server startup.

method.name: packet_size
method.digest: Set the OSC chunk size in floats (128 – 16384, default: 10240)
method.description: Controls how many float values are packed into each UDP packet. Smaller values reduce per-packet fragmentation risk over the internet.

method.name: verbose
method.digest: Enable (1) or disable (0) timing event log
method.description: When enabled, prints a timeline of events to the Max console: [PREDICT] trigger timestamp, [SEND] start and done times, [RX] first chunk and complete times, [WRITE] buffer write duration and total cycle time.

method.name: live_mode
method.description: In normal mode (0), prediction is skipped when curr=0 since there is no past context available yet. In live mode (1), curr=0 is treated as a buffer cycle point — the last p*T samples of the buffer are used as context, allowing seamless prediction across loop boundaries.

method.name: test_packet
method.digest: Send a test OSC packet and measure round-trip time

method.name: print
method.digest: Send a print command to the Python server

method.name: reset
method.digest: Send a reset command to the Python server

method.name: get_client_ip
method.digest: Fetch and display this machine's public IP address

entry.name: Buffer Coordinate System
entry.description: All positions are in samples. T is set once per session (matches model input size). p and w can change between predictions. curr advances with your playback cursor. The object handles clamping to buffer bounds.

entry.name: Network Configuration
entry.description: Default ports: sender 7000, listener 8000. Receive buffer is 4 MB to handle bursts of prediction chunks. For remote servers, smaller packet_size values reduce fragmentation risk.

entry.name: Python Server Protocol
entry.description: The server sends /ready true when ready. Prediction results are returned as OSC messages addressed by stem name (e.g. /bass, /drums) carrying chunk_idx, total_chunks, and float data. The stem names on the server are configured in server.py and must match the names given to set_buffer. Completion is driven by chunk reassembly, not by any separate signal. The server ignores all OSC messages while a model is loading.

attribute.name: verb
attribute.digest: Enable or disable verbose timing log (0 = off, 1 = on)
attribute.description: Same as sending the verbose message.
